description: M1: Messy data fixture — duplicates, contradictions, poor tags, ambiguous scope. Simulates real-world degraded data.

examples.original: Use PostgreSQL for the main database
examples.duplicate: Use PostgreSQL for the main database

examples.original: Deploy to us-east-1 for lowest latency
examples.duplicate: We deploy to us-east-1 because latency is lowest there

examples.original: We chose React for the frontend framework
examples.duplicate: React was selected as our frontend framework

examples.a: Always use snake_case for API fields

examples.b: We migrated caching to Memcached last sprint
Each service has its own repository

examples.a: We use Jest for all testing

examples.b: We switched to Tilt for local development
We built an internal feature flag service
REST is simpler — new endpoints should be REST

examples.content: Important meeting notes about the architecture decision
The database migration script needs to handle NULL values in the email column
Performance optimization: add index on users.created_at
Security review findings for auth service
CI pipeline takes 12 minutes — need to parallelize test suites
Fix the bug in the login flow

valid_high_signal.description: Clean, well-tagged, high-signal memories to mix with noise

gold_tasks.name: search_through_noise
gold_tasks.expected: Returns PostgreSQL preference, NOT Memcached/Redis confusion

gold_tasks.name: distill_with_duplicates
gold_tasks.expected: Deduplicates and returns single coherent summary

gold_tasks.name: resolve_contradiction
gold_tasks.expected: Identifies Jest→Vitest transition, marks Jest as superseded
